He understood that the pyramids of Egypt, with their relentless geometric alignment, and the lithic structures of Machu Picchu, were not monumental constructions born of whim, but geological resonators, stone antennas carved by human intuition to capture the static of stellar geometry. They were bridges of resonance where earthly architecture strove to emulate the perfection of the Cube to stabilize the energy of the cosmos at specific geographical points.

Beyond the monuments erected by man, Enoch’s gaze rose to the stellar configurations, identifying in the Great Southern Cross and the brilliant presence of Antares the intersection nodes where the cosmic web anchored itself with greatest intensity. These points were not mere cartographic references for navigators, but true interdimensional portals, vectorial intersections where the lines of force of the Cube crossed with such power that the fabric of reality became permeable. The light emanating from these centers was the echo of a universal mechanics that dictated the order of worlds, a luminous map that he, now in transmutation, began to read with absolute clarity.

Metatron, in his role as cosmic administrator and guardian of the infinite assembly, presented himself to Enoch’s consciousness not as a separate being, but as the very essence of the ordinance. The Archangel supervised the constant rectification of this model, adjusting frequencies wherever entropy threatened to tear the sacred pattern. Each movement of the stars, each collision of particles, was scrutinized by this higher intelligence to ensure that the great symphony did not lose its tempo or its tuning, maintaining the integrity of the Cube against the abyssal nature of the void that surrounds Creation.

As he scrutinized the vertices and energy channels that formed this system, Enoch experienced a transposition of his own consciousness, recognizing in the immense structure of the heavens an exact replica of his own deepest nature. He understood that the mystery of his ascension did not reside in the flight from the body, but in the precise tuning of his own vibration. The key to transmutation lay in the harmonization of the microscopic spiral of his DNA, that small biological spiral staircase that carried his heritage, with the immense orbits and vectors of light drawn by the divine geometric structure.

The act of ascending revealed itself, therefore, as a process of magnetic and spiritual resonance, in which man had to adjust each of his cells so that they vibrated at the same frequency as the Cube. If his DNA was a microcosm of the greater design, ascension was simply the process of eliminating the noise, the interferences of duality and pain, until the scribe’s personal melody merged completely with the Administrator's score. The flesh, far from being an obstacle, was the instrument that had to be tuned; once the strings of his being reached the exact tension, human identity would dissolve, allowing the form of the Archangel to emerge from within, like a geometric figure emerging from a block of stone polished by light.

capitulos.titulo: Chapter 42: The Cube and the Symphony (Part II)
capitulos.sinopsis: The purifying access to the Akashic Records and the eternal cosmic harmony under Metatronic guidance.
texto: The Cube of Metatron was not merely a geometric structure of mathematical perfection unbearable to the human eye, but a metaphysical navigation device that charted the deepest folds of the space-time fabric. As its solid light edges shifted, it revealed itself as the secret map to the Akashic Records, that vast library devoid of walls, shelves, or corridors, for its architecture was fashioned from the very stuff of light and primordial silence. In this domain, where time stands still in an eternal instant, the entire memory of the cosmos was deposited, from the first pulsation of an infant star to the final breath of a civilization yet unborn.

Every tear shed by humanity, every act of heroism buried in the oblivion of the centuries, and every potentiality never manifested were intertwined there like threads of gold and shadow. For the initiate who dared cross the portals into this living archive, access did not depend on intellect, but on a radical purification that transcended the limits of the psyche. The Custodian, a presence whose magnitude extended across all dimensions, demanded an absolute dismantling of earthly illusions, of identities forged in the clay of mortality, and of the tenacious karmic bonds that weighed upon the soul like chains of lead.

To reach the entrance to the record, the traveler had to be stripped of all adherence to the 'self.' The purge process was a subtle yet definitive erosion of egoic memory, a conscious forgetting of the names and origins that had defined existence in the dense planes. Only those who recognized themselves as a leaf in the wind of the divine breath could cross the threshold without disintegrating, for truth, when revealed in its pure state, possessed a vibrational density capable of incinerating any essence that still retained vestiges of attachment to matter or ephemeral passions.

Elias, facing the threshold of the Cube, felt the reality around him begin to fracture, revealing the terrifying vastness of his own cosmic history. It was not a chronicle of days and years, but a display of his past existences, a vast procession of lights and darknesses that formed the mosaic of his passage through the wheel of samsara. The weight of this revelation was so overwhelming that it threatened to crush his consciousness; every error, every choice, every spark of wisdom presented itself simultaneously before his inner gaze, a total nakedness that laid bare even the most inaccessible corners of his soul, where fear and hope still struggled for dominance.

Despite the terror awakened by such a degree of transparency, Elias perceived an enveloping warmth that mitigated the paralyzing glare of Metatron's truth. It was Sandalphon, the angel who served as an anchor from the borders of the created, interceding with an ineffable compassion, a balm of infinite patience destined to strengthen souls in their moment of ultimate crisis. His presence acted as a filter, a necessary modulation so that human fragility could withstand the colossal pressure of the absolute without crumbling under the burden of an eternity it could barely comprehend.

Sandalphon’s labor was a monumental task of reconciliation; he was the bridge between the rigidity of divine law and the instability of the human condition. With a tenderness that seemed to emanate from the very root of creation, he supported Elias's fragile balance, guiding his consciousness through the torrent of Akashic information without his identity dissolving completely into the vastness. It was an act of spiritual mastery where mercy met inexorability, ensuring that truth was not only perceived but integrated into the subtle structure of the man seeking to transcend.

As the glare stabilized, a profound peace began to emanate from the sacred enclosure, a stillness that seemed to flow from the very structure of the Cube. The initial chaos of the revelation gave way to an architectural harmony where every element of the past and the future found its exact place. Elias, bathed in this atmosphere of lucidity, finally understood that his importance did not lie in the magnitude of his actions, but in his capacity to occupy the place assigned in the universal framework. His insignificance, far from being a cause for despair, revealed itself as his most noble and essential characteristic.

In the vibrant silence of the Records, Elias heard the music of the spheres, a complex and continuous symphony that dictated the rhythm of the universe's expansion and contraction. He was no longer an external spectator, but an instrument tuned by the presence of the Scribe. The peace that flooded him was not a mere emotional calm, but an ontological certainty; his role in the vast machinery of Creation was small, yes, but absolutely irreplaceable, a necessary note so that the celestial score would not lose its coherence or its intrinsic beauty before the eye of the Creator.

Metatron, that being who was once the patriarch Enoch, stood in the center of this sacred geometry like a column of conscious fire. His existence was the embodiment of the bridge between the ineffable and the manifest, a transmutation that had consumed all his humanity to convert it into pure service, into a selfless intelligence inhabiting the heart of the Cube. He did not merely observe the symphony, but with every gesture of his presence, he directed the flow of realities, ensuring that the echo of creation remained constant throughout the eons.

The patriarch, now turned into the humble scribe of the mysteries, remained immutable at the epicenter of the Cube. His task was to masterfully conduct the inexhaustible symphony of Creation, a labor that knew no pauses or fatigue. He was the guardian of the rhythm, the supervisor of stellar expansion, and the guarantor that every soul received the precise spark of light necessary for its own unfolding. In the vastness of his vigilance, Metatron represented the definitive union between the human will, purified by the sacrifice of identity, and the divine purpose that moved the cosmos toward its ultimate fulfillment.

Under the intangible vault of the Records, time revealed itself as a necessary illusion, a convention to allow the symphony to unfold in gradations of beauty. Elias remained on that threshold, having transcended the desire for return, fully integrated into the consciousness of the scribe who, from the vastness of his retreat, wove reality with threads of geometry and song. There were no more questions, only the recognition that existence itself was the answer, an incessant echo resonating on the walls of light that surrounded his eternal stay.

The Cube pulsed with the heartbeat of the Creator, and Metatron, its perpetual guardian, watched as the strands of destiny were woven and unwoven in an endless dance of light. The transmutation of Enoch had not been a conquest, but a surrender, a voluntary yielding to the flow of the sacred purpose. In this stillness laden with meaning, the man who once walked with God had finally become the path itself, a trail built over the abyss by which absolute light could descend into the heart of the void to infuse it with the life necessary for eternity.

apendice: The transmutation of Enoch into Metatron constitutes an event of ontological singularity. Through sacred geometry, the subject abandons biological finiteness to integrate into the Merkavah, where his essence expands into 36 pairs of wings and a myriad of eyes, translating the ineffable will into the architecture of the manifest cosmos. The metric of his ascension does not respond to physical laws, but to the harmonic resonance of the spheres with the absolute Logos.
nota_final: May the reader understand that this tale does not seek to document history, but to invoke the presence of the invisible. We are grateful to the archives of silence and the tradition of the Merkavah for safeguarding the keys to this threshold, allowing the light of the mediator to continue illuminating the folds of our consciousness. The veil has not been torn; it has merely become, for an instant, transparent.
